summary: Iteration 7 backend tests: JS grader (correct/broken/timeout), Python grader regression, application `source` attribution, time-to-hire endpoint, master-admin QB CRUD & permissions, and import-from-custom-module. All 10 new tests pass. Regression across iterations 3-6: 59/60 pass; the single failure (test_iteration4::test_schedule_without_calendly_400) is a pre-existing HR-state issue unrelated to iter7 (a calendly_url is already saved so the endpoint returns 200 instead of 400).

minor.issue: Pre-existing iteration_4 test test_schedule_without_calendly_400 fails because a calendly_url is already persisted. Not an iter-7 regression, but worth noting — test needs to clear calendly_url first or the endpoint should not silently accept when unset.

action_items: Consider clearing calendly_url in a fixture for test_iteration4::test_schedule_without_calendly_400, or make the endpoint idempotent w.r.t. shared state.
critical_code_review_comments: code_grader.grade_javascript wraps test_code in an async IIFE — correct for `await` usage, but SIGKILL from Node OOM (--max-old-space-size=256) will be reported as returncode!=0 rather than a specific memory error; consider labeling.
server.py time_to_hire loads all applications + all invites + all submissions into memory (up to 5000). Fine at current scale but should paginate/aggregate at scale.
server.py _resolve_questions iterates every custom module doc every import — an index/lookup by q.id would be cleaner.

Master QB CRUD accepts arbitrary `questions` list without Pydantic validation of question shape (mcq weights, correct_index bounds). A malformed question could break import/exam. Recommend nested models.
ApplyIn.source is trimmed to 40 chars and lowercased — good; but no enum whitelist which could clutter by_source dashboards.
test_credentials: HR: hr@cohortdata.com / Cohort@2026 ; Master: darshan@cohortdata.com / MasterCohort@2026
seed_data_creation: Test creates ephemeral jobs, applications, candidates and custom modules. Custom modules used in tests: soft-deleted (CRUD test) and cleaned up (import test). CRUD test's created module is deleted as part of the DELETE step.
context_for_next_testing_agent: JS grader wraps test_code in `(async () => { ... })()` and prepends `const assert = require('assert');`. Coding tasks require `test_code` with `assert.*` calls; empty test_code = manual review. Submission responses expose `submission_id`; details via GET /api/hr/submissions/{id}. Custom modules: question `id` is stored as-provided (unlike seeded ones which are auto-prefixed by module_id::). So import from custom uses the raw q.id.
